article_meta.title: Crisis Leadership: Using Technology To Maintain Stability And GrowthByPhil Portman,Forbes Councils Member.for

reasoning: The summary stays neutral, attributes claims to the author, avoids adopting promotional or loaded phrasing from the op-ed, and reflects the single perspective present. No unsubstantiated assertions or subjective judgments are stated as fact.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (explores, introduces, advocates) and attributes them to the author/article.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Presents recommendations as the author’s views rather than settled facts.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative ideas are clearly framed as Portman’s or the article’s stance.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarmist language; tone is measured.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about unspoken intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Single-opinion piece accurately represented without injecting counter-arguments not in the source.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: All relevant elements of the lone perspective are included.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Descriptors like ‘cutting-edge’ appear only in attributed context.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced and descriptive; neither overemphasizes risks nor hype.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No undue deference to elites or dismissal of other groups; it mirrors the source’s single perspective.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the source by softening absolute claims and emphasizing attribution.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Properly lists the perspective (Forbes Technology Council via Phil Portman) without omitting others present (none).
